upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft does add latent take-up and a second instrumental exposure map, but the mandatory parent-corner reduction is not yet exact because the current target and deterministic policy object do not cleanly collapse to the parent's ADE LP.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: finite joint-completion LP sharpness for network-exposure ADE bounds

published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference identifies complier-type effects with instrumental exposure mappings; schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid gives sharp misspecified-exposure bounds without the IV compliance layer

nibbering_oosterveen_2025_iv_interference_policy partially identifies policy-relevant IV effects under interference; auerbach_auerbach_tabord_meehan_2024_exposure_policy_relevance warns exposure effects need not be policy-useful

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: strict cellwise-relaxation nonsharpness from shared hidden-edge compatibility

published_prior_art.cite: savje_2024_misspecified_exposure_mappings separates exposure estimands from assumptions but does not give this IV-network policy nontransport witness

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The Imai-Jiang-Malani and Vazquez-Bare bibliography entries have incorrect title/issue/page metadata relative to the verified JASA records.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: PN4 is only partially delivered: delta_summary promises a parent reduction, but §9 does not yet state an exact projection/corner in which the current target equals the parent ADE bound.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship object K_g and the quotient set T(P) are promised but not computed in §9 on a worked instance; as written this caps tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The Nibbering-Oosterveen and Auerbach-Auerbach-Tabord-Meehan comparators are cited only at paper level, with no section/equation/page anchor tying them to the transport gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The counterexample targets independently stitched product relaxations but names no published estimator or workflow that actually uses that relaxation.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The four-unit exhibit computes only the demand vector V_g, not a full observable law with equal complier projections and disjoint sharp policy intervals from §6 primitives.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed complier direct/spillover formulas do not weight or condition on the complier event C_i(s,t,G), so the stated target is not yet a complier effect.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The parent reduction equates deterministic policy rule g with the randomized assignment design and does not explicitly project away the extra spillover/policy coordinates.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Once quotient preservation is assumed as existence of K_g, the support-function interval is mostly projection/support-function algebra rather than three nontrivial steps.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement indexes policy intervals by latent structures A and B even though the sharp identified interval should be a function of P and the maintained restrictions.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch does not specify the observable law, take-up tables, or equality of exposure-indexed complier projections needed for the claimed open-class nontransport.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite the §6 complier estimands with explicit principal-stratum indicators and complier-share denominators, then restate Theorem 1 for that corrected target.
Add a §9 exhibit computing K_g, T(P), and the policy support-function endpoints from a complete four-unit observable law, not just from graph-demand vectors.
Replace the product-stitching counterexample target with a named published workflow or downgrade Conjecture 2 to a diagnostic corollary rather than a flagship counterexample.
Repair the parent-corner corollary by stating an exact coordinate projection under perfect compliance and trivial instrumental exposure, with g removed or defined as a fixed assignment vector.

recommended_next_step: Revise before Stage 0: fix the complier target, make the parent reduction exact, and compute the promised quotient/policy objects in §9.
